name: tradingview_grid3
title: TradingView Supercharts Chart Grid And Right Price Scale Builder Spec

include: current price line and current price marker
resize behavior relevant to the chart grid
exclude: watchlist and right panels except as layout constraints
bottom date-range toolbar outside the chart widget

prompt_engineering_guide_principles_used: decompose the task into precise subtasks
measure live behavior instead of relying on memory
use structured output with explicit constraints and uncertainties
iterate observations after interactions mutate chart state

evidence_collected: confirmed chart canvases and surrounding UI presence

tools_used.limitation: TradingView static bundle request failed in this environment with host resolution blocking.

tools_used.builder_warning: Do not depend on TradingView private globals in an implementation. Use the behavioral model and formulas below.

canvas_backing_store.observed: canvas width and height matched CSS width and height at DPR 1
canvas_backing_store.builder_rule: At DPR > 1, keep CSS geometry in layout state and multiply backing canvas dimensions by devicePixelRatio.

desktop_side_panel_open_variant.builder_rule: The chart widget fills the available center area. When adjacent panels consume width, plot width shrinks while price scale and time scale heights remain stable.

viewport_estimate.measurement_status: estimated from default Playwright new-tab chart geometry

compact_default_new_tab_variant.observation: Right price-scale width was 72px in this compact state, not 80px.

narrow_resized_manual_state.state_caveat: Measured after manual/log price-scale interactions; geometry is valid, price range is not an autoscale baseline.

layout_rules_for_builder.chartWidgetHeight: availableChartHeight excluding out-of-scope bottom toolbar if present
layout_rules_for_builder.priceScaleWidth: auto-sized between observed 72px and 80px based on viewport, labels, and scale state

desktop_fresh_layer_inventory.role: price tick labels and current price marker base layer

price_tick_relationship.inference: Horizontal gridlines align to round price values. With a 46,262 USDT visible range, about 34px per gridline corresponds to about 1,989 USDT, rounded to a nice 2,000 USDT tick step.

vertical_gridlines_desktop.behavior: Vertical gridlines are generated by the time-axis tick provider. Their pixel gaps vary because calendar ticks are selected by visible range and label density, while candle centers remain uniformly spaced in logical bar coordinates.

grid_density_rules_for_builder.horizontal: Choose a nice price step so gridline spacing is roughly 32px to 40px. Use 1-2-5 style steps scaled by powers of 10.
grid_density_rules_for_builder.vertical: Choose calendar ticks that maintain readable labels, then map each tick's logical bar/time position to x.

log_scale.status: observed accidentally during one desktop manual-scale test; not primary baseline
log_scale.builder_rule: When log scale is active, map log(price) linearly: y = (log(priceMax) - log(price)) / (log(priceMax) - log(priceMin)) * plotHeight.
log_scale.uncertainty: Detailed log-scale tick generation was not deeply analyzed because the requested emphasis was right scale behavior, and the clean price-axis drag test was captured in linear mode.

right_price_labels.format: Use the instrument price formatter. BTCUSDT daily rendered examples like 67,890.12 and 66,824.95.
right_price_labels.label_text_alignment: left-aligned inside the right price scale with small horizontal padding
right_price_labels.overlap_rule: Do not allow tick labels to overlap hover/current price markers; markers paint above base tick labels.

desktop_baseline_example.interpretation: There were about 10 logical bars of right margin beyond the latest visible bar.

time_to_x.builder_rule: For a trading series, map each candle by logical bar index rather than elapsed wall-clock seconds. For BTCUSDT daily, logical bars align closely to consecutive days, but the renderer should still use a data-index/logical-coordinate model.
time_to_x.timeTickRule: Time labels and vertical gridlines are chosen from calendar ticks, then converted to logical x coordinates.

body_width_rule.status: estimated from visual/canvas sampling; exact TradingView rounding not confirmed

volume_overlay.position: inside the plot pane near the bottom
volume_overlay.height_rule_estimate: about 16 percent of plot height with a practical minimum around 60px
volume_overlay.builder_rule: Volume bars share candle x centers and body widths, but use a separate volume-to-y scale anchored to the plot bottom.

right_margin.builder_rule: Keep latest real candle about scrollPosition * barSpacingPx left of the right plot edge when pinned to latest.

behavior.line: A horizontal dotted/dashed line is drawn across the plot at the latest price.
behavior.marker: A right-axis price marker is drawn at the same y coordinate and above regular tick labels.
behavior.color: Uses the latest price direction/series color; green was observed for BTCUSDT during the inspection.
behavior.updates: Marker text and line y update live with quote changes.

distinction_from_hover_price_label.hover_price_label: black/neutral and only visible during plot hover

crosshair.horizontal_duty_observation: 590 nontransparent pixels on a 1255px row, roughly 47 percent duty
crosshair.vertical_duty_observation: 365 nontransparent pixels on a 791px column, roughly 46 percent duty

plot_hover_desktop_example.legend_behavior: The top-left in-chart legend switched from latest values to the nearest candle under the crosshair; observed text included O/H/L/C/Vol values for the hovered candle.

price_axis_hover.observed: Moving the pointer over the price-axis area alone did not draw the plot crosshair or hover time label in the compact test; transparent overlay canvases remained empty.
price_axis_hover.builder_rule: Only plot hover should create the full crosshair. Price-axis hover may reveal scale mode controls but should not create a time-axis hover label by itself.

time_axis_hover.observed: Time-axis interactions belong to horizontal scale control. Plot crosshair labels are tied to plot hover, not time-axis-only hover.
time_axis_hover.uncertainty: Time-axis-only hover styling was not deeply sampled beyond drag behavior.

plot_drag_horizontal.behavior: Pans the logical time range. Plot geometry, right price scale width, and price range remain unchanged while autoscale data extrema remain the same.

direction.drag_right: moves the visible range earlier; logical left/right decrease; scrollPosition can become negative when panned away from latest
direction.drag_left: moves the visible range later; logical left/right increase; scrollPosition increases

math.note: Observed shifts were close but not exactly equal to deltaX/barSpacing, likely due event thresholds, snapping, and momentum-free drag rounding.

measured_examples.name: plot_drag_down_clean_desktop
measured_examples.result: No visible range, logical range, or price range change while autoscale was active.

time_axis_drag_horizontal.behavior: Changes horizontal zoom/bar spacing rather than panning. The right edge/latest-side scroll position stayed fixed in the measured test.

measured_drag_right.priceRangeInterpretation: Y-axis autoscale expanded because the time zoom-out included a broader price history.

time_axis_drag_horizontal.builder_rule: Dragging the time scale right should zoom out. Dragging left is expected to zoom in by symmetry, but the left-drag direction was not separately measured in this pass.

wheel_zoom_plot.behavior: Mouse wheel over the plot zooms the time scale around the current/pinned right-side context while preserving scrollPosition in the measured latest-pinned state.

right_price_scale_vertical_drag.behavior: Vertical dragging on the right price scale changes the y-scale. It does not pan time, resize the chart, or move the price axis. It disables auto scale and reveals price-scale mode buttons.

direction.drag_down_positive_dy: expands the visible price range; pricePerPixel increases; chart zooms out vertically
direction.drag_up_negative_dy: contracts the visible price range; pricePerPixel decreases; chart zooms in vertically

desktop_manual_measurement.state_caveat: A later desktop resize test was in manual/log state after a control click, so use it only to confirm direction and geometry invariance.

estimated_scale_formula.anchor: approximately the price under the initial mouse-down y coordinate
estimated_scale_formula.status: estimated from compact linear and desktop manual samples

right_price_scale_horizontal_drag.behavior: Horizontal dragging on the right price scale did not change visible price range, time range, plot geometry, or price-scale width in the measured compact state.
right_price_scale_horizontal_drag.builder_rule: Do not implement horizontal right-axis dragging as an axis-width resize unless separately required. Treat vertical movement as the scale gesture and horizontal-only movement as no-op for chart ranges.

states.name: idle
states.description: No pointer over plot; base grid, series, current price marker, and axes are visible.

states.name: plot_hover
states.description: Pointer over plot; crosshair and hover labels are visible; legend reflects nearest candle under pointer.

states.name: plot_drag_pan
states.description: Pointer down and horizontal move over plot; time range pans.

states.name: time_axis_drag_zoom
states.description: Pointer down on bottom time scale and horizontal move; bar spacing changes.

states.name: price_axis_hover
states.description: Pointer over right price scale; scale controls may appear. Plot crosshair is not created by price-axis-only hover.

states.name: price_axis_drag_scale
states.description: Pointer down on price scale and vertical move; y range scales around the pointer anchor and auto scale is disabled.

states.name: manual_price_scale
states.description: Price axis has been manually scaled; auto scale button is not activated and mode buttons may be visible on hover.

states.name: auto_price_scale
states.description: Y range is recomputed from visible series extrema plus padding whenever the time range changes.

resize_behavior.chart_widget: Fills available center area after surrounding TradingView chrome is excluded.
resize_behavior.plot: Recomputed as chartWidget minus priceScaleWidth and timeScaleHeight.
resize_behavior.time_scale: Height stayed 28px in all measured states.
resize_behavior.price_scale: Width varied between 72px and 80px. Treat as auto-sized with a minimum rather than a constant.
resize_behavior.logical_range: When resizing while pinned to latest, the right logical edge and scrollPosition can remain fixed while the left logical edge changes to fit the new plot width and bar spacing.
resize_behavior.manual_price_scale: Manual price scale can persist through resize; auto scale must be explicitly restored by user action.

builder_acceptance_criteria: The chart grid must be a canvas-backed plot plus separate right price scale and bottom time scale bands.
At a 1440x900 desktop viewport with surrounding chrome excluded, reproduce a chart region approximately 1335x819, plot approximately 1255x791, right axis 72-80px, and time axis 28px.
Horizontal gridlines should be 1px and about 34px apart in the BTCUSDT daily baseline, with tick values snapped to round price steps.
Vertical gridlines should follow the time tick provider and are not required to be uniformly spaced in pixels.
Price-to-y and y-to-price must follow the linear formulas in this file when log scale is off.
Candle centers must be placed by logical bar index and barSpacingPx, not by raw elapsed pixel-per-second alone.
Plot hover must display a dashed crosshair, a right-axis hover price label centered on pointer y, a bottom hover time label centered on pointer x, and nearest-candle legend values.
Plot horizontal drag must pan time without changing price scale geometry.
Plot vertical-only drag should not move the chart while autoscale is active.
Time-axis horizontal drag and wheel zoom must change barSpacing and visible logical bars.
Right price-scale vertical drag must scale the price range, disable auto scale, and reveal manual scale controls.
Right price-scale horizontal drag must not resize the axis or alter ranges unless future evidence contradicts this pass.
The current price marker must stay aligned with the current price line using the same price-to-y mapping.

edge_cases.behavior: The chart widget narrows, but the internal chart layout formula remains plot = widget - priceAxis - timeAxis.
Right price scale may shrink to 72px. Do not assume 80px is universal.
Auto scale remains disabled after a price-axis drag. Time-range changes no longer necessarily recompute the same y range as auto mode.
Y price range may expand indirectly because autoscale includes a broader set of candles.

edge_cases.case: price axis hover without plot hover
edge_cases.behavior: Do not show a full plot crosshair solely because the pointer is over the right price scale.
CSS geometry should stay in CSS pixels while canvas backing dimensions scale by DPR.

uncertainties.detail: The body width rule is visually inferred as about 65 percent of bar spacing with integer rounding.
The exponential factor k ~= 1.6 was derived from two vertical drag samples. TradingView may clamp or adjust this under other symbols, log scale, or extreme ranges.
Drag-right was measured as zoom out. Drag-left is documented as expected zoom in by symmetry but was not independently measured.
Log scale was accidentally activated in a desktop manual test; the JSON includes the standard log mapping but does not fully specify TradingView log tick generation.
Unauthenticated TradingView access limited live inspection to a single visible chart pane; multi-pane splitters and pane maximize behavior remain out of this pass.
Browser-use opened a dark/loading TradingView chart with side panel visible. It was not used for numeric measurements.
Camoufox could not resolve a TradingView static bundle in this environment, so Playwright is the primary verified browser tool.
